items.title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
items.summary: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.

items.title: Oratomic raises $300M to build a viable quantum computer that needs only 20K qubits
items.summary: The new startup raised $300 million co-led by ARCH Venture Partners, Spark Capital, and Khosla Ventures.

items.title: I spent a week using the Trump phone — it sucks
items.summary: The Trump phone was never a serious phone. Not when it was announced last June, in dodgy renders and with an incoherent spec sheet. Nor when Trump Mobile admitted - just two weeks later - that it wouldn't be made in the US. Not even when the company revealed the final phone, first to me […]

items.title: EU threatens Meta with fines over addictive features on Facebook and Instagram
items.summary: The tech giant is in breach of the Digital Services Act by focusing on features like infinite scroll, autoplay, push notifications, and the highly personalized recommendation algorithms, the European Commission said.

items.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
items.summary: Open source AI is booming, according to Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue. The company has grown into something like a GitHub for AI in recent years, where AI builders can share and download open models and datasets, now used by roughly half the Fortune 500. Delangue has seen the same story play out again and again: companies start […]

items.title: Instagram’s Adam Mosseri: If you don’t like AI, ‘then you shouldn’t have it in your feed’
items.summary: Though Instagram head Adam Mosseri doesn't want to filter out AI content on the platform, he argues that you "shouldn't have it in your feed" if you don't like it. "I don't think we should filter out AI content," Mosseri said during an interview on Lenny Rachitsky's podcast. "I think we should let you know […]

items.title: Would you host part of an AI data center in your home?
items.summary: A solar and home energy storage company is expanding into AI data centers, but not by building one - instead, it's offering to pay its customers to put its compute units in their homes. Sunrun is launching a pilot program for a new "distributed AI compute" program that will "place numerous compute nodes in homes […]

items.title: Doom’s new expansion shows just what makes id Software special
items.summary: This week should have been a celebration for Doom developer id Software. The studio just launched Revelations, a meaty expansion for Doom: The Dark Ages that adds a powerful new weapon and more demonic levels to blast through. Even though I hadn't picked up The Dark Ages for more than a year, it only took […]

items.title: Polestar owners left ‘holding the bag’ after EV brand pulls out of the US
items.summary: Last month, Polestar shocked the auto industry when it announced that it was pulling out of the US. The EV company's decision came after the federal government denied its authorization to continue selling its cars despite a rule banning vehicles with Chinese-made connected vehicle software. Polestar, which is headquartered in Sweden but majority owned by […]

items.title: A New Experiential Gallery Just Might Change Your Mind About AI Art
items.summary: Billed as the “world’s first museum of AI arts,” Dataland uses wearables and troves of material from the Amazon to merge nature, biometrics, and art.

items.title: How Deutsche Telekom is rewiring telecommunications with AI
items.summary: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

items.title: Robot Dogs, Teslas, and Rescue Helicopters: The UN AI Summit Was a Lot
items.summary: Amid live coding sessions and Silicon Valley optimism, the UN’s AI for Good summit wrestled with an urgent question: Can global governance catch up before the technology races beyond its control?

items.title: After Apple, India’s smartphone manufacturing boom enters new phase with Vivo JV
items.summary: Vivo's joint venture could become a template for Chinese smartphone makers in India.

items.title: OpenAI says GPT 5.6 is the ‘preferred model’ for Microsoft Copilot 365 amid breakup chatter
items.summary: OpenAI's new family of models will continue to power Microsoft's suite of workplace and productivity apps.
